team_members.name: Stani Kulechov
team_members.notes: Finnish entrepreneur; studied law at University of Helsinki; founded ETHLend in 2017, rebranded to Aave in 2018. Highly active on X/Twitter (@StaniKulechov). Publicly known face of the protocol.

team_members.name: Jordan Lazaro Gustave
team_members.notes: Co-founder; operational lead since ETHLend days. Based in London.

team_members.name: David Truong
team_members.notes: Joined Aave Companies leadership; oversees business development and institutional relationships.

team_members.name: Emilio Frangella
team_members.notes: Core protocol engineer; GitHub handle @EmilioFrangella; key contributor to Aave V3 architecture.

team_members.name: Marc Zeller
team_members.role: Founder, Aave Chan Initiative (ACI) — Governance Delegate
team_members.notes: Left Aave Companies to run ACI, the largest independent governance delegate. Highly influential in DAO governance. Active on X (@lemiscate).

team_members.name: Nadia Alvarez
team_members.notes: Previously led institutional partnerships; current status unclear from public sources.

investors.notes: Participated in LEND/Aave token rounds; disclosed in multiple public interviews. Exact % NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER.
3AC collapsed June 2022; any AAVE holdings were liquidated by liquidators. No longer an active holder.
Listed as early backer on Crunchbase free page for Aave. Exact terms NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER.
Noted in public Crunchbase listing. Exact terms NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER.
Disclosed in public DeFi coverage; participated in early AAVE ecosystem rounds.
2017 ICO raised ~$16.2M for 1B LEND tokens. All LEND migrated to AAVE at 100:1 ratio in 2020. Fully vested/liquid since migration.

~3M AAVE (~30% of 16M max supply) held in Ecosystem Reserve controlled by AAVE governance DAO. Released via governance votes for grants, liquidity mining, safety module incentives. Source: Aave tokenomics docs.

legal_events.description: Aave Companies Ltd registered and operating under UK FCA scrutiny. The UK Financial Services and Markets Act 2023 brought DeFi protocols under broader regulatory perimeter. Aave has not received a formal FCA enforcement action but operates in a jurisdiction with active DeFi regulatory development.

SEC sued Binance and Coinbase (July 2023), listing several tokens as unregistered securities. AAVE was NOT named in either complaint. However, the broader SEC enforcement posture against DeFi governance tokens creates indirect legal risk for AAVE token classification.

CFTC issued guidance and enforcement actions against DeFi protocols (Opyn, ZeroEx, Deridex). Aave was NOT named but the CFTC explicitly stated on-chain lending protocols may fall under its jurisdiction if they offer leveraged products. Aave's flash loans and variable-rate borrowing are under ongoing interpretive scrutiny.

OFAC sanctioned Tornado Cash smart contracts. Aave DAO governance subsequently debated and implemented front-end blocking of OFAC-sanctioned addresses. No direct action against Aave, but the event forced protocol-level compliance changes.

EU MiCA (Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation) passed; full enforcement begins Dec 2024. Aave's governance token and lending protocol will need to assess compliance. Aave Companies has London HQ but EU operations may require licensing. No enforcement action yet.

SEC EDGAR search for 'Aave' returns NO filings — Aave Companies has not registered any securities with the SEC, has not filed Form D (Reg D exemption), and has no 13F institutional filings under the Aave name. This means either (a) token sales were structured to avoid US securities law or (b) filings exist under a different entity name not publicly linked.

founder_credibility_summary: Stani Kulechov is one of DeFi's most credible and consistently doxxed founders, with a verifiable legal/tech background, continuous public presence since 2017, and a track record of shipping multiple protocol versions (V1→V2→V3) and expanding to GHO stablecoin. No fraud allegations, rug-pull history, or regulatory actions have been directed at him personally as of the research date.
vc_overhang_summary: VC overhang risk is LOW-to-MODERATE: early backers (Framework, Blockchain Capital, Parafi) received tokens during 2017–2020 at prices far below current levels, and given the 2020 LEND→AAVE migration and subsequent bull market, most institutional positions are likely fully liquid and substantially distributed. The largest single overhang is the DAO Ecosystem Reserve (~3M AAVE, ~30% of supply), which is released only via governance vote, providing a structural brake on dumping.
alignment_summary: Insider and retail incentives are MODERATELY ALIGNED but with caveats. The DAO governance model means large AAVE holders (including VCs and the Aave Chan Initiative delegate) have outsized voting power, creating plutocratic risk. However, the Safety Module requires insiders to stake AAVE (subject to slashing), directly tying their capital to protocol solvency — a genuine skin-in-the-game mechanism. The GHO stablecoin launch and Aave V3 revenue-sharing proposals suggest the team is focused on long-term protocol value accrual rather than short-term token extraction. Key misalignment risk: Ecosystem Reserve grants can be directed to insiders via governance without full transparency.
sources: https://defillama.com/protocol/aave — DefiLlama Aave TVL and protocol data
